page: Amazon Web Services m7i.12xlarge is a general-purpose server powered by the Nitro hypervisor and an Intel Xeon 8488C processor. This x86_64 architecture system features 48 vCPUs, 24 physical cores with 2 threads per core, and a dedicated CPU allocation running at 3.2 GHz. The processor includes a 105 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for tile and int8 operations. It is equipped with 192.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 5 GHz, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core. The instance does not include local storage or GPUs, and it features a baseline network bandwidth of 19 Gbps.

In benchmark evaluations, the m7i.12xlarge demonstrates strong memory and processing capabilities. Its DDR5 memory subsystem achieves top 25% performance in both cached and uncached read/write memory bandwidth tests. Computational benchmarks, including Geekbench single-core and multi-core tests, place the server in the top 25%, with notable results in ray tracing, image processing, and text processing. Cryptographic and database operations also rank in the top 25%, highlighted by a Passmark CPU encryption score of 51,341.08 and a Redis SET throughput of 63.12 million operations per second. Additionally, the hardware shows top 10% tier performance in LLM text generation for small models, alongside strong top 25% performance for medium and large model prompt processing.

From a resource density perspective, the m7i.12xlarge offers qualitative cost efficiency by balancing a standard 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio with high-speed DDR5 memory and specialized hardware acceleration flags like AMX and AVX-512. This configuration allows the server to handle vector and matrix math workloads efficiently without the added cost of dedicated GPU hardware. The lack of local storage requires reliance on network-attached storage, which represents a tradeoff for I/O-heavy tasks. This server is optimized for enterprise applications, database operations, static web serving, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
description: Amazon Web Services m7i.12xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 48 vCPUs and 24 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8488C processor running at 3.2 GHz. Utilizing the Nitro hypervisor, this x86_64 system includes 192.0 GB of DDR5 memory at 5 GHz, but lacks local storage and GPUs. It delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 19 Gbps. Benchmark results place the instance in the top 25% for memory bandwidth, Geekbench multi-core execution, and database operations, with a top 10% tier ranking in small-model LLM text generation. The processor supports advanced instruction sets like Intel AMX and AVX-512, enhancing its efficiency for matrix and vector calculations. This server is suited for enterprise applications, database operations, static web serving, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m7i.12xlarge is a general-purpose server with 48 vCPUs, 192 GB DDR5 RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor, delivering strong database and CPU-based machine learning performance.

tagline: A balanced general-purpose server featuring Intel Xeon processors, high-speed DDR5 memory, and advanced matrix acceleration for enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 48 vCPUs and 24 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8488C processor
192.0 GB of high-speed 5 GHz DDR5 memory
Intel AMX and AVX-512 instruction support for accelerated matrix operations
Top 25% benchmark performance in memory bandwidth and database operations
19 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with Nitro hypervisor integration
Suited for enterprise applications, database hosting, and CPU-based LLM inference
